autoUpdate.prompt.t: Automatically download and apply climon updates in the background? [y/N]
autoUpdate.prompt.hint: Yes/no onboarding prompt asking the user to opt in to background auto-updates; default is No. Preserve the [y/N] hint convention (capital N marks the default).

boot.app_insights_logging_disabled.hint: Warning logged during dashboard server startup when Application Insights cannot initialize; {err} is the initialization error and the server falls back to local logging.

cli.command_invocation.hint: Debug log emitted when a climon subcommand is invoked; {subcommand} is the fixed subcommand keyword (e.g. run, server, config), never the user-supplied command a session executes.

cli.stream_output.hint: Debug log mirroring user-facing CLI output; {stream} identifies stdout or stderr and {detail} is the emitted text chunk.

daemon.attention_fingerprint_mismatch.hint: Trace diagnostic logged while clearing attention when the stored and current screen fingerprints differ; {attentionFp} is the prior attention fingerprint and {currentFp} is the current fingerprint.

daemon.clearing_attention_status.hint: Debug log emitted when the daemon clears a session's needs-attention state; {source} is detector or user and {fingerprint} says whether the screen fingerprint changed.

daemon.needs_attention_status.hint: Debug log emitted when the daemon marks a session as needing attention; {reason} explains why and {fingerprint} is the current screen fingerprint.

daemon.sampled_fingerprint_changed.hint: Trace diagnostic from the idle sampler when a status transition occurs after the screen fingerprint changed; {status} is the new status and {prev} and {curr} are the old and new fingerprints.

daemon.sampled_fingerprint_unchanged.hint: Debug log from the idle sampler when a status transition occurs without a screen fingerprint change; {status} is the new status.

discovery.checking_peer_home.hint: Debug message while inspecting a configured peer CLIMON_HOME; {peerHome} is the peer home directory path being checked.

discovery.discovering_dashboard.hint: Debug message emitted when dashboard discovery begins before checking local state and peer endpoints.

discovery.found_local_dashboard.t: found local dashboard: pid={pid} port={port} ingest={ingest}
discovery.found_local_dashboard.hint: Debug message confirming a live local dashboard was found; {pid} is its process ID, {port} is its HTTP port, and {ingest} is the local ingest port or 'none'.

discovery.local_server_pid_not_alive.t: local server.json exists (pid={pid}) but process not alive
discovery.local_server_pid_not_alive.hint: Debug warning that a local server beacon exists but its process is dead; {pid} is the recorded dashboard server process ID.

discovery.no_peer_home_configured.t: no peerHome configured, no dashboard found
discovery.no_peer_home_configured.hint: Debug message indicating discovery stopped because no peerHome setting is configured after no local dashboard was found.

discovery.no_peer_ingest_found.t: no peer ingest.json found (peer ingest not running?)
discovery.no_peer_ingest_found.hint: Debug message indicating the configured peer home has no active ingest beacon, so the peer ingest service may not be running.

discovery.no_reachable_peer_host_found.hint: Debug message indicating all candidate peer hosts were probed and none could be reached.

discovery.peer_host_probe_result.hint: Debug line for one peer host TCP probe; {host} is the candidate hostname or address, {port} is the ingest port, and {reachability} says whether it was reachable.

discovery.peer_ingest_state.hint: Debug message showing the peer ingest beacon contents; {port} is the advertised ingest port and {host} is the advertised host or 'unset'.

discovery.probing_peer_host_candidates.hint: Debug message before probing peer hosts; {candidates} is the comma-separated list of hostnames or addresses to try.

ingest.client_disconnected_cleanup.hint: Debug message logged when a remote ingest client disconnects and its sessions are cleaned up; {clientId} is the remote client identifier and {sessionCount} is how many sessions will be torn down.

ingest.client_idle_destroying_channel.hint: Debug message logged when an ingest client has been silent too long and the channel is closed; {clientId} is the remote client identifier and {idleTimeout} is the idle limit in milliseconds.

ingest.evicting_previous_connection.hint: Debug message logged when a reconnect replaces an existing ingest connection; {clientId} identifies the remote client whose older connection is being closed.

ingest.hello_received.hint: Debug message logged after the ingest daemon receives the initial hello handshake; {clientId} is the remote client identifier from that handshake.

ingest.inbound_connection.hint: Debug message logged for a new TCP connection to the ingest daemon; {host} and {port} are the peer address and port reported by the socket.

ingest.listening.hint: Debug message logged when the ingest daemon starts listening for remote uplink connections; {host} and {port} are the bind address and {pid} is the daemon process ID.

ingest.loopback_bind_failed.t: warning: could not bind loopback 127.0.0.1:{port} after {attempts} attempts ({code})
ingest.loopback_bind_failed.hint: Warning logged when the extra loopback listener for dev tunnels could not be opened; {port} is the loopback port, {attempts} is the retry count, and {code} is the bind error code.

ingest.loopback_bind_retry.t: loopback bind attempt {attempt}/{maxAttempts} failed ({code}), retrying in {delay}ms...
ingest.loopback_bind_retry.hint: Debug message logged before retrying a failed loopback bind for dev tunnels; {attempt} is the current try, {maxAttempts} is the limit, {code} is the error code, and {delay} is the wait in milliseconds.

ingest.loopback_listener_added.hint: Debug message logged when a secondary loopback listener is added for dev tunnel forwarding; {port} is the local loopback port now accepting tunnel traffic.

ingest.mux_decode_error_from_client.t: mux decode error from client {clientId}, destroying channel
ingest.mux_decode_error_from_client.hint: Error logged when a remote client sends invalid mux data and its channel is destroyed; {clientId} identifies the offending client or is unknown before handshake.

ingest.port_unavailable_using_alternativ.hint: Debug message logged when the requested ingest port is busy and another port is chosen; {port} is the original port and {newPort} is the replacement.

ingest.remote_session_file_deleted.t: sessions watcher: remote session file deleted externally: {localId}
ingest.remote_session_file_deleted.hint: Debug message logged when the sessions watcher sees a remote session metadata file removed externally; {localId} is the local namespaced session ID being dismissed.

ingest.resolved_bind_address.hint: Debug message logged after resolving where the ingest daemon should bind; {host} is the chosen interface and {port} is the configured starting port.

ingest.session_added.hint: Debug message logged when a remote client advertises a session to materialize locally; {sessionId} is the remote session ID, {displayCommand} is its shown command, and {status} is its current state.

ingest.session_removed.hint: Debug message logged when a remote client reports that a session ended or disappeared; {sessionId} is the remote session ID to disconnect locally.

ingest.session_updated.hint: Trace message logged when a remote session metadata update is applied; {sessionId} is the remote session ID and {patch} is the JSON patch being sanitized and stored.

ingest.signed_control_rejected.t: signed control message rejected for clientId={clientId}: {reason}
ingest.signed_control_rejected.hint: Warning logged when a signed remote-control frame fails verification and is rejected; {clientId} identifies the remote client when known, and {reason} describes the verification failure.

ingest.singleton_acquired_daemon.hint: Debug message logged when this process obtains the singleton lock and proceeds to run the ingest daemon; there are no placeholders.

ingest.singleton_already_running.t: another ingest instance is already running (pid={pid}), exiting
ingest.singleton_already_running.hint: Debug message logged when another ingest daemon already holds the singleton lock and this process exits; {pid} is the existing daemon process ID.

launcher.ensure_uplink_config.t: ensureUplink: enabled={enabled} host={host} tunnelId={tunnelId} port={port} peerHome={peerHome}
launcher.ensure_uplink_config.hint: Debug log emitted while the launcher reads remote uplink settings; {enabled}, {host}, {tunnelId}, {port}, and {peerHome} show the resolved configuration values or set/unset markers.

launcher.ensure_uplink_plan.hint: Debug log emitted after planning whether to start the remote uplink bridge; {shouldSpawn} says if it will start and {warningSuffix} notes whether a warning was shown.

launcher.launch_banner.hint: Informational launch banner when climon starts monitoring a session; {version} is the climon version and {id} is the session identifier.

launcher.uplink_not_spawned.hint: Debug log emitted when the launcher decides not to start the remote uplink bridge; there are no placeholders.

launcher.uplink_spawning_detached_proces.hint: Debug log emitted when the launcher starts the remote uplink bridge as a detached process; there are no placeholders.

push.send_failed.hint: Warning logged when sending a web push notification fails for an active subscription; {endpoint} is the subscription URL and {err} is the send error.

server.climon_server_listening.t: climon server v{version} listening on http://127.0.0.1:{port}/
server.climon_server_listening.hint: Info log when the dashboard HTTP server is bound and ready; {version} is the climon version and {port} is the loopback TCP port.

server.climon_server_shutting_down.hint: Info log when the dashboard server begins graceful shutdown; {reason} explains the trigger such as a signal or internal HTTP request.

server.climon_server_starting.hint: Info log emitted at the beginning of dashboard server startup.

server.closing_dashboard_listener_stream.hint: Debug log during shutdown while the HTTP listener, SSE streams, and dashboard tunnel are being closed.

server.live_daemon_disconnect_reconcile_.t: failed to reconcile disconnected local session {sessionId}: {error}
server.live_daemon_disconnect_reconcile_.hint: Warning when a failed dashboard-to-daemon socket cannot be reconciled into disconnected metadata; {sessionId} identifies the local session and {error} is the read, probe, or patch failure.

server.find_existing_dashboard_probe.hint: Debug log before checking for an already-running dashboard; {url} is the base dashboard URL whose health endpoint will be probed.

server.find_existing_health_probe_failed.hint: Debug log when the existing-dashboard health probe throws; {error} is the probe failure message.

server.find_existing_health_response.hint: Debug log after the health probe response; {ok} is the HTTP success flag and {healthy} says whether the JSON body reported ok true.

server.find_existing_healthy_server_foun.hint: Debug log when an existing healthy dashboard is detected; {pid} is the live process id or unknown if no live pid was found.

server.find_existing_not_healthy.t: findExistingDashboardServer: not healthy — no existing server
server.find_existing_not_healthy.hint: Debug log when the health check shows there is no usable existing dashboard server.

server.handle_existing_dashboard_state.hint: Debug log before resolving a conflict with an already-running dashboard; {existing} is serialized server URL and PID data and {tty} says whether stdin is interactive.

server.handle_existing_http_shutdown_att.hint: Debug log before attempting graceful shutdown via the internal HTTP endpoint; {url} is the existing dashboard base URL.

server.handle_existing_http_shutdown_suc.hint: Debug log when the existing dashboard server was stopped through the internal HTTP endpoint.

server.handle_existing_non_interactive_e.hint: Debug log when startup cannot prompt in a noninteractive terminal and leaves the existing dashboard running.

server.handle_existing_pid_stop_attempt.hint: Debug log before trying to stop an existing dashboard by process id; {pid} is the target server process id.

server.handle_existing_pid_stop_failed.hint: Error log when PID-based termination of the existing dashboard server did not succeed.

server.handle_existing_pid_stop_succeede.hint: Debug log when PID-based termination of the existing dashboard server succeeded.

server.handle_existing_termination_faile.hint: Error log when all attempted methods to terminate the existing dashboard server failed.

server.handle_existing_user_confirmed.hint: Debug log when the user confirms that the existing dashboard server should be terminated.

server.handle_existing_user_declined.hint: Debug log when the user chooses not to terminate the existing dashboard server; {answer} is the normalized prompt response.

server.ingest_daemon_already_running.hint: Debug log when the remote ingest daemon is already alive and correctly bound; {pid} is its process id and {port} is its listening port.

server.ingest_daemon_bootstrap_failed.hint: Warn log when the remote ingest daemon cannot be started during server startup; the dashboard still comes up. {error} is the failure message.

server.ingest_daemon_ready.hint: Debug log when a newly spawned ingest daemon becomes live; {pid} is its process id and {port} is its listening port.

server.ingest_daemon_recycling_stale.t: ingest daemon is stale or wrong-bound; recycling it...
server.ingest_daemon_recycling_stale.hint: Debug log when an existing ingest daemon is stale or bound to the wrong host and will be restarted.

server.ingest_daemon_starting.hint: Debug log before spawning the detached remote ingest daemon.

server.internal_shutdown_accepted.hint: Debug log when the loopback-only internal shutdown endpoint accepts a request; {address} is the requester IP address.

server.internal_shutdown_rejected_non_lo.hint: Warning log when the internal shutdown endpoint rejects a non-loopback request; {address} is the requester IP address.

server.persist_dashboard_tunnel_enabled_.hint: Debug log when saving the persisted Tunnel Link enabled flag fails; {enabled} is the desired boolean value and {error} is the failure message.

server.plain_shutdown_complete.hint: Debug log after graceful shutdown cleanup has completed.

server.plain_shutdown_hard_watchdog.t: plainShutdown: hard watchdog fired; forcing process.exit(0)
server.plain_shutdown_hard_watchdog.hint: Debug log when shutdown cleanup exceeds the hard timeout and the process will force-exit.

server.plain_shutdown_ingest_stop_failed.hint: Error log during graceful shutdown when stopping the colocated ingest daemon throws; {error} is the failure message.

server.plain_shutdown_ingest_stop_result.hint: Debug log during graceful shutdown after attempting to stop the colocated ingest daemon; {stopped} indicates whether it stopped.

server.plain_shutdown_leave_ingest_demot.t: plainShutdown: leaving ingest shutdown to its demotion path
server.plain_shutdown_leave_ingest_demot.hint: Debug log during demotion-initiated shutdown when the ingest daemon is left to finish its own exit path.

server.plain_shutdown_triggered.hint: Debug log when graceful shutdown starts; {pid} is the server process id, {reason} is the trigger, and {path} is the server state file being removed.

server.push_notify_failed.hint: Error log when the push service fails to send an attention notification; {error} is the failure message.

server.push_service_unavailable.hint: Error log when the push service cannot initialize; {error} is the initialization failure message.

server.request_shutdown_health_non_ok.t: requestServerShutdownViaHttp: health probe returned non-ok — server is down
server.request_shutdown_health_non_ok.hint: Debug log while polling after a shutdown request when the health endpoint returns non-OK, meaning the server is down.

server.request_shutdown_health_threw.t: requestServerShutdownViaHttp: health probe threw — server is down
server.request_shutdown_health_threw.hint: Debug log while polling after a shutdown request when the health probe throws, meaning the server is down.

server.request_shutdown_http_polling.t: requestServerShutdownViaHttp: POST returned 200; polling for shutdown
server.request_shutdown_http_polling.hint: Debug log after the internal shutdown POST succeeds and the client begins polling for the server to exit.

server.request_shutdown_http_post_failed.hint: Error log when the HTTP request to the shutdown endpoint fails; {error} is the request failure message.

server.request_shutdown_http_posting.hint: Debug log before POSTing to the internal shutdown endpoint; {shutdownUrl} is the full endpoint URL.

server.request_shutdown_http_status_fail.t: requestServerShutdownViaHttp: POST returned {status} — treating as failure
server.request_shutdown_http_status_fail.hint: Error log when the shutdown endpoint returns an unsuccessful status; {status} is the HTTP status code.

server.request_shutdown_timeout.t: requestServerShutdownViaHttp: timed out waiting for server to stop
server.request_shutdown_timeout.hint: Error log when the server remains reachable beyond the shutdown wait window.

server.server_state_writing.hint: Debug log before writing the server state file; {path} is the destination path and {content} is the serialized state JSON with pid and port data.

server.server_state_written.hint: Debug log after the server state file has been written successfully.

server.settle_dual_promote_no_peer.t: settleDualPromote: no competing peer server; staying host
server.settle_dual_promote_no_peer.hint: Debug log when the cross-OS dual-promote settle window finds no competing peer server, so the local server remains host.

server.settle_dual_promote_peer_decision.hint: Debug log when dual-promote compares local and peer servers; {decision} is the win or lose outcome and {basis} explains the tie-break reason.

server.settle_dual_promote_started.hint: Debug log at the start of the dual-promote settle window; {localIsWsl} identifies the local OS, {localStartedAt} is the local start timestamp, {peerHome} is the peer CLIMON_HOME path, and {settleMs} is the wait duration.

server.settle_dual_promote_write_peer_sh.t: settleDualPromote: writing shutdown request to peerHome={peerHome}
server.settle_dual_promote_write_peer_sh.hint: Debug log when the local server wins dual-promote and writes a shutdown request for the peer; {peerHome} is the peer CLIMON_HOME path.

server.settle_dual_promote_write_self_sh.t: settleDualPromote: LOSING — writing self-shutdown request to {climonHome}
server.settle_dual_promote_write_self_sh.hint: Debug log when the local server loses dual-promote and writes a shutdown request to itself; {climonHome} is the local CLIMON_HOME path.

server.shutdown_request_watcher_started.t: shutdown-request watcher started (no ingest, peer configured)
server.shutdown_request_watcher_started.hint: Debug log when the server starts a filesystem watcher for peer shutdown requests because no ingest daemon is running.

server.shutdown_request_watcher_valid_re.t: shutdown-request watcher: received valid request from {requestedBy}; invoking shutdown
server.shutdown_request_watcher_valid_re.hint: Debug log when the shutdown-request watcher receives an authorized peer request; {requestedBy} identifies the peer side that requested shutdown.

server.shutdown_step_timeout.t: warning: {label} did not finish within {ms} ms; continuing shutdown.
server.shutdown_step_timeout.hint: Debug warning log when one shutdown substep takes too long; {label} names the substep and {ms} is the timeout in milliseconds.

server.startup_log_detail.hint: Debug log for granular dashboard server startup progress; {detail} is the current startup step or status message.

server.stop_dashboard_ingest_stop_failed.hint: Debug log when stopDashboardServer cannot stop the associated ingest daemon; {error} is the failure message.

server.stop_dashboard_ingest_stop_result.hint: Debug log after stopDashboardServer attempts to stop the associated ingest daemon; {ingestStopped} indicates whether it stopped.

server.stop_dashboard_read_live_pid.hint: Debug log after reading the current live dashboard pid; {pid} is the process id or undefined when no live server was found.

server.stop_dashboard_terminate_pid_resu.hint: Debug log after attempting PID-based dashboard termination; {pid} is the target process id and {result} says whether it exited.

server.tunnel_link_reestablish_failed.t: Tunnel Link could not be re-established: {message}
server.tunnel_link_reestablish_failed.hint: Warning log when a persisted dashboard Tunnel Link cannot be restored; {message} is the failure explanation shown to the user.

server.tunnel_link_reestablished.hint: Info log when a persisted dashboard Tunnel Link is restored; {url} is the public tunnel URL.

shell.detect_debug.hint: Debug log from parent-shell detection; {detail} is the diagnostic message about the detected or fallback shell.

shell.detect_trace.hint: Trace log from parent-shell detection; {detail} is a fine-grained diagnostic message about process-tree probing.

teardown.beacon_retained_owner_alive.t: beacon retained; owning process still alive
teardown.beacon_retained_owner_alive.hint: Warning that a beacon file was left in place because its owner still appears alive; {path} is the beacon file path and {pid} is the owner process ID.

teardown.dashboard_server_stopped.hint: Debug message confirming the dashboard server stopped; {pid} is the dashboard server process ID.

teardown.failed_to_stop_daemon.t: failed to stop daemon during teardown
teardown.failed_to_stop_daemon.hint: Warning that teardown could not stop a daemon; {daemon} names the component, {pid} is its process ID, and {reason} explains the failure.

teardown.ingest_daemon_stopped.hint: Debug message confirming the ingest daemon stopped; {pid} is the ingest daemon process ID.

teardown.local_dashboard_stack_teardown_.hint: Debug summary after local dashboard stack teardown; {serverStopped}, {ingestStopped}, and {uplinkStopped} are stop results, while {removed}, {failures}, and {staleFiles} are counts.

teardown.removed_beacon.hint: Debug message confirming a stale or safe-to-remove beacon file was deleted; {path} is the removed beacon path.

teardown.starting_local_dashboard_stack_.hint: Debug message emitted when teardown starts for the local dashboard server, ingest daemon, uplink daemon, and related beacon files.

teardown.stopping_dashboard_server.hint: Debug message before attempting to stop the dashboard server; {pid} is its process ID and {port} is its HTTP port.

teardown.stopping_ingest_daemon.hint: Debug message before attempting to stop the ingest daemon; {pid} is the daemon process ID.

teardown.stopping_uplink_daemon.hint: Debug message before attempting to stop the uplink daemon during full teardown; {pid} is the daemon process ID.

teardown.stopping_uplink_daemon_via_pidf.hint: Debug message before stopping an uplink daemon found from the pidfile; {pid} is the daemon process ID.

teardown.uplink_daemon_stopped.hint: Debug message confirming the uplink daemon stopped; {pid} is the uplink daemon process ID.

telemetry.example_seed.hint: Catalog bootstrap marker used when seeding telemetry messages; it is informational and has no placeholders.

telemetry.prompt.t: Help improve climon by sending anonymous usage telemetry? [y/N]
telemetry.prompt.hint: Yes/no onboarding prompt asking the user to opt in to anonymous usage telemetry; default is No. Preserve the [y/N] hint convention (capital N marks the default).

update.applied.t: Update applied. Start new sessions (or restart the server) to use {next}.
update.applied.hint: Confirmation printed after an update is applied successfully; {next} is the newly installed version the user will get after restarting.

update.banner.hint: One-line banner printed at session start when a newer version is available; {current} is the running version and {next} is the available version. Keep the command `climon --update` verbatim and untranslated.

update.deferredLocked.t: Update could not be applied right now (files in use). Will retry later.
update.deferredLocked.hint: Message printed when an update is deferred because target files are locked or in use; the update will be retried automatically later.

update.failed.hint: Error printed when `climon update` throws unexpectedly; {detail} is the underlying error message, which may contain filesystem paths, so it is redacted before telemetry transmission.

update.upToDate.t: climon is already up to date ({current}).
update.upToDate.hint: Message printed by `climon update` when no newer version exists; {current} is the already-installed version.

update.verifyFailed.t: Update aborted: signature verification failed. No changes were made.
update.verifyFailed.hint: Error printed when an update is rejected because its cryptographic signature could not be verified; nothing was installed.

uplink.auth_rejected_bridge_closed.t: auth rejected after bridge closed, stopping
uplink.auth_rejected_bridge_closed.hint: Error logged when dev tunnel authentication is detected as rejected after an uplink bridge closes, telling translators this is a stopping condition.

uplink.auth_rejected_port_wait.t: auth rejected during port wait, stopping
uplink.auth_rejected_port_wait.hint: Error logged when dev tunnel authentication is rejected while waiting for the forwarded port, telling translators the uplink will stop.

uplink.bridge_connected.hint: Informational debug log emitted when the uplink bridge opens and sends its hello frame; {clientId} is the stable remote client identifier and {keepAlive} is the ping interval in milliseconds.

uplink.channel_error.hint: Error logged when the active uplink socket reports a channel failure; {message} is the low-level socket error text.

uplink.channel_idle_destroying_bridge.hint: Informational debug log emitted when the mux channel has been silent too long and will be closed; {idleTimeout} is the idle limit in milliseconds.

uplink.channel_teardown.hint: Informational debug log emitted while shutting down an uplink bridge; {reason} explains why it ended, and {bytesSent} and {bytesReceived} are traffic counters.

uplink.client_id.hint: Informational debug log that records the stable uplink identity; {clientId} is the generated or configured client identifier.

uplink.connection_error.hint: Error logged after an uplink connection attempt fails; {message} is the failure text and {code} is the Node or socket error code, or none.

uplink.devtunnel_connect_spawning.t: spawning devtunnel connect for tunnel {tunnelId}, forwarding port {port}
uplink.devtunnel_connect_spawning.hint: Informational debug log emitted before starting devtunnel forwarding; {tunnelId} is the Microsoft dev tunnel identifier and {port} is the forwarded port.

uplink.direct_target.hint: Informational debug log showing a configured direct remote ingest target; {host} is the target hostname and {port} is its TCP port.

uplink.ingest_requested_attach.hint: Informational debug log emitted when the ingest side asks to attach to a local session; {sessionId} identifies that session.

uplink.ingest_requested_detach.hint: Informational debug log emitted when the ingest side asks to detach from a local session; {sessionId} identifies that session.

uplink.initial_reconcile_done.t: initial reconcile done, advertised {advertisedCount} session(s), sent {bytesSent} bytes
uplink.initial_reconcile_done.hint: Informational debug log after the bridge advertises current local sessions; {advertisedCount} is the number of sessions announced and {bytesSent} is the bytes written so far.

uplink.mux_decode_error.hint: Error logged when incoming mux data cannot be decoded and the uplink channel is destroyed.

uplink.no_reachable_target_resolved.hint: Informational debug log when no uplink destination can be selected; {detail} says whether this is a retry because a peer may start later or a final exit.

uplink.no_remote_target_configured.t: no remote target configured (no peerHome, no enabled tunnel/direct), exiting
uplink.no_remote_target_configured.hint: Informational debug log emitted when uplink has no peer, tunnel, or direct target configured and exits without connecting.

uplink.port_not_reachable.hint: Informational debug log when the selected ingest endpoint cannot be reached; {host} and {port} identify it and {reason} explains the likely cause.

uplink.port_reachable_connecting.hint: Informational debug log emitted after a target port probe succeeds and before connecting; {host} is the endpoint hostname and {port} is its TCP port.

uplink.reconnecting.hint: Informational debug log emitted before retrying a failed uplink connection; {backoff} is the delay in milliseconds.

uplink.resolved_peer_target.hint: Informational debug log when peer discovery finds a reachable ingest target; {host} is the peer hostname and {port} is the ingest port.

uplink.singleton_acquired_supervisor_loo.hint: Informational debug log emitted after this process wins the uplink singleton lock and starts the supervisor retry loop.

uplink.singleton_already_running.t: another uplink instance is already running (pid={pid}), exiting
uplink.singleton_already_running.hint: Informational debug log emitted when another uplink process already holds the singleton lock; {pid} is that process ID.

uplink.tcp_channel_established.t: TCP channel established, starting mux bridge
uplink.tcp_channel_established.hint: Informational debug log emitted when the TCP connection to ingest succeeds and the mux bridge is about to start.

uplink.tunnel_port_not_found.t: could not discover port for tunnel {tunnelId} (no port mapping found)
uplink.tunnel_port_not_found.hint: Informational debug log emitted when a dev tunnel has no discoverable port mapping; {tunnelId} is the tunnel being inspected.

uplink.waiting_for_port.t: waiting for port {host}:{port} to become reachable...
uplink.waiting_for_port.hint: Informational debug log emitted while polling for the selected ingest endpoint to accept connections; {host} is the endpoint hostname and {port} is its TCP port.
